data.content:
Now I have all the info. Let me create the `Directory.Packages.props` and update the project files to remove `Version` attributes.

Packages with highest versions:
- Azure.Identity: 1.13.2
- Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting: 8.0.1
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.0
- System.Text.Json: 10.0.1
- xunit: 2.9.3

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me identify the packages and their highest versions:

- System.Text.Json: 10.0.1 (Api, Shared, Tests), 8.0.4 (Worker) → highest: 10.0.1
- Azure.Identity: 1.13.2 (Api), 1.11.4 (Shared) → highest: 1.13.2
- Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting: 8.0.1 (Worker) → 8.0.1
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.0 (Worker) → 1.15.0


- xunit: 2.9.3 (Tests) → 2.9.3

Now I need to create a Directory.Packages.props file with centralized version management enabled and add all these package versions to it, then strip out the Version attributes from each project file. First, let me check if this file already exists.

    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
